title: macrothelypteris polypodioides
intro: Macrothelypteris polypodioides, also known as the southern maidenhair fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Macrothelypteris polypodioides include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared for herbal use by infusing its leaves in hot water to make a tea, or by using its roots and leaves in traditional medicine preparations, including poultices and infusions.
